briefdescription.para: Handles the core stencil for own vol.

detaileddescription.para: vector of meshEntryVolumes for abutting surfaces, needed to compute diffusion rates across junctions. Indexed from zero.

briefdescription.para: Handles stencil for core + abutting voxels.

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned int index, const vector< double > &entry, const vector< unsigned int > &colIndex)
memberdef.name: addRow

briefdescription.para: Inherited virtual function to clear the vector of MeshEntryVolume.
Virtual function to return volume of mesh Entry, including.

detaileddescription.para: Utility function to return volume of any voxel including those diffusively coupled and aubtting the present volume.

briefdescription.para: Add boundary voxels to stencil for cross-solver junctions.

detaileddescription.para: extendStencil adds voxels to the current stencil m_, to build up a monolithic stencil that also handles the entries just past all the boundaries. This function may be called many times to deal with the addition of multiple junctions. Before the first of these calls, the m_ matrix should be set to the coreStencil_.

inbodydescription.para: Organizes vj by voxel, that is, by row.

briefdescription.para: Looks up stencil to return vector of indices of coupled voxels.

detaileddescription.para: Virtual function to return info on Entries connected to this one.

briefdescription.para: Returns entire sparse matrix of mesh. Used by diffusion solver.

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned int meshIndex, const double **entry, const unsigned int **colIndex) const
memberdef.name: getStencilRow

detaileddescription.para: Derived function to return SparseMatrix-style row info for specified mesh entry.
Looks up stencil to return vector of diffusion coupling to neighbor voxels.

The MeshCompt represents the base class for all compartments which are set up as a meshes. These include CubeMesh, CylMesh and NeuroMesh. The MeshCompt handles the stencil that is the core of all the meshes. The stencil specifies which compartments diffuse into each other, and by how much.
